title: Gaza Six-Month Ceasefire Assessment: 'Neither War Nor Peace'
detail: On April 10, 2026 — marking roughly six months since the October 2025 Gaza ceasefire agreement — Al Jazeera and the Washington Post published major assessments describing a fragile stalemate: violence was reduced but not stopped, humanitarian conditions remained dire, and no meaningful political stabilization had occurred. Al Jazeera's report, titled 'Neither war nor peace: What Gaza looks like six months into ceasefire,' documented continued IDF incursions, Gaza Government Media Office records of 2,073+ Israeli ceasefire violations, and Hamas violations including 139 confirmed incidents per the IDF. The Washington Post framed the ceasefire as a potential template for post-Iran-war de-escalation. NPR also published an interview with Netanyahu's foreign policy adviser confirming Israel's hardline stance on disarmament. The anniversary coincided with the collapse of the Board of Peace disarmament deadline.

media.caption: Gaza six months into ceasefire: Neither war nor peace

title: Board of Peace Faces Severe Cash Crunch: Only $1B of $17B Pledged Received
detail: On April 10, 2026, Newsmax and Brussels Morning reported that Trump's Board of Peace (BoP) Gaza reconstruction framework was facing a severe cash crunch — with less than $1 billion of the $17 billion pledged by donor nations having been actually deposited into BoP accounts. The funding shortfall was attributed to donor hesitation stemming from the ongoing Iran-Israel regional war, which had diverted diplomatic and financial attention away from Gaza reconstruction. Without reconstruction funding, analysts warned the economic incentive for Hamas to accept disarmament terms would be essentially eliminated. The BoP's governance structure — which grants the Trump administration near-total control with minimal oversight — was also cited as a deterrent for European donors. The funding crisis directly threatened the viability of Phase Two of the ceasefire framework.

media.caption: Board of Peace cash crunch threatens Gaza reconstruction framework
